components.role: Inverting amplifier — gain stage with LED feedback clipping
Positive-swing clipping LED — clips at ~1.8V, higher headroom than silicon
Feedback resistor — sets pre-clip gain with Ri
Input resistor — sets gain and input impedance

dsp_model.description: Gain into soft clipper with higher knee voltage than silicon; more headroom before clipping onset

behavioral_description: LED clipping in the feedback loop provides a more open, dynamic overdrive compared to silicon diodes. The higher forward voltage (~1.8V vs ~0.6V) means the signal has three times more headroom before clipping begins, preserving more of the original dynamics and pick attack. The result is a crisper, more articulate overdrive that retains note clarity even at higher gain settings. The clipping knee is slightly sharper than silicon, giving a bit more edge and presence. This is the mod many players make to Tube Screamer-style circuits for a more transparent, less compressed drive sound. LEDs of different colors have slightly different thresholds (red ~1.8V, green ~2.0V, blue ~3.0V), enabling further tuning.
